preamble: The shift from the voiceless uvular stop [q] to the glottal stop [ʔ] is a defining characteristic of many modern urban Arabic dialects. This change marks a significant reduction in the place of articulation from the back of the mouth to the throat.

examples.note: Standard /q/ is realized as [ʔ] in Cairene and other urban dialects.

sources: Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic. Oxford University Press.
Holes, C. (2004). Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties. Georgetown University Press.
